pr.title: A few easy_func fixes, mostly Yoshi related
pr.author: Gelatart

summary: Matched four small previously stubbed easy_func functions, mostly in Yoshi-related fighter and item code. The PR implemented Yoshi SpecialN helper fn_8012E44C, event Yoshi egg spawn logic, and two thrown Yoshi egg item helpers, while tightening nearby header prototypes. The decomp.dev report credited +288 matched bytes and four new 100% function matches.
what_changed: Replaced the fn_8012E44C stub in ftYs_SpecialN.c with a matched implementation that gets the Fighter from the gobj, calls ftCommon_8007D7FC, changes motion state to 0x16C with flags 0x0C4C5082U using fp->cur_anim_frame, then calls fn_8012E3B4_inline and qwe.
Implemented it_3F14_Logic42_Spawned in itevyoshiegg.c by retrieving the Item with GET_ITEM, zeroing it->xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xDD4, and calling it_8029B268.
Added it/inlines.h include to itevyoshiegg.c so GET_ITEM could be used.
Implemented it_802B2890 in ityoshieggthrow.c by clearing item->owner, clearing item->xDC8_word.flags.x13, and calling Item_8026A8EC.
Implemented itYoshieggthrow_UnkMotion1_Coll in ityoshieggthrow.c as a collision helper that calls it_8026E5A0(gobj, it_802B2C38) and returns false/0.
Added includes for it/it_266F.h and it/item.h in ityoshieggthrow.c to support the newly matched helper calls and item field access.
Updated itevyoshiegg.h prototypes: gave it_3F14_Logic42_Spawned an explicit gobj parameter name and changed it_8029B268 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool it_8029B268(Item_GObj* gobj).
Updated ityoshieggthrow.h prototype for it_802B2C38 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void it_802B2C38(HSD_GObj*).
smart_moves: Used existing inline accessors GET_FIGHTER and GET_ITEM rather than open-coding object data access, keeping the implementations consistent with surrounding decomp style.

Converted unknown prototypes to concrete signatures only where the new callsites provided evidence: it_8029B268 takes Item_GObj* and returns bool, and it_802B2C38 is a void HSD_GObj* callback suitable for passing to it_8026E5A0.
Preserved matching-sensitive constants in fn_8012E44C, including motion state 0x16C and flag mask 0x0C4C5082U, instead of prematurely inventing higher-level names.

For it_3F14_Logic42_Spawned, documented uncertainty around the itemVar union member with the comment 'This should be EvYoshiEgg' while still using the currently available bombhei union path needed to access xDD4.
Targeted several isolated stubs in one small PR, yielding measurable progress without broad refactors.
decomp_lessons: Small easy_func matches often depend on correcting nearby function signatures and includes, not just filling in the function body.
When a callback is passed to another item helper, its required signature can sometimes be inferred from the callee callsite; here it_802B2C38 became void it_802B2C38(HSD_GObj*).
Item union field names may lag behind behavioral knowledge. If the storage is known but the named variant is questionable, leave a precise uncertainty comment rather than renaming without evidence.
Motion-state helper functions can match by preserving the exact current-animation-frame handoff and hard-coded motion flags from assembly.

Clearing owner and item flag fields before invoking an existing item lifecycle/helper routine is a reusable pattern for item reset/despawn-style helper matches, but the exact semantics should not be overclaimed from this PR alone.
naming_conventions: Fighter object variables use gobj for Fighter_GObj* and fp for Fighter*.
Item object variables use item_gobj or gobj for Item_GObj* and item or it for Item*.
Still-unknown functions retain address-style names such as fn_8012E44C, it_802B2890, it_8029B268, and it_802B2C38.
Behaviorally grouped item motion functions use names like itYoshieggthrow_UnkMotion1_Coll and itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion0_Anim/Phys/Coll.
Header declarations include address comments such as /* 29B1AC */ and concrete parameter names where known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For fn_8012E44C, matched the sequence of common fighter state setup, Fighter_ChangeMotionState with exact constants, inline helper call, and qwe(gobj).
For itYoshieggthrow_UnkMotion1_Coll, matched a compact collision wrapper by calling it_8026E5A0 with a callback and returning 0.
For it_802B2890, matched direct item field clearing followed by an existing item helper call, requiring inclusion of item.h for Item_8026A8EC.
For it_3F14_Logic42_Spawned, matched item variable initialization plus delegation to it_8029B268; GET_ITEM required adding it/inlines.h.
decomp.dev reported four new 100% matches: fn_8012E44C (+144 bytes), it_802B2890 (+56 bytes), it_3F14_Logic42_Spawned (+44 bytes), and itYoshieggthrow_UnkMotion1_Coll (+44 bytes).
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report showed GALE01 matched code increased to 38.57%, +0.01%, with +288 bytes and four new matches.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or specialize xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xDD4 for EvYoshiEgg.
Find PRs touching fn_8012E3B4_inline or qwe in ftYs_SpecialN.c to clarify the semantics of fn_8012E44C.
Search for subsequent signature changes to it_8029B268 and it_802B2C38 to confirm the inferred prototypes remained stable.
Inspect other item collision wrappers using it_8026E5A0(callback) for reusable matching patterns.
